weeks_1_to_8.why: Build the classification map first: reasoning, tool use, alignment, foundation models.

weeks_9_to_24.why: Use the map to interpret current model and product updates.

monthly_review_week.why: Reconnect paper knowledge to live AI market and model shifts.

core_papers.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
core_papers.why_it_matters: It explains the Thought → Action → Observation loop that makes Claude Code feel like an agent rather than a chatbot.
guide_questions: What does ReAct add on top of Chain-of-Thought?
Why is Observation necessary after Action?
Where do you see ReAct behavior in Claude Code?
What failure mode does ReAct reduce compared with reasoning-only prompting?
If you were designing a PR-writing agent, what would Thought, Action, Observation be?
core_papers.business_translation: An agent is not just a model that thinks. It is a loop that reasons, uses tools, observes results, and updates its next step.

core_papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
core_papers.why_it_matters: It formalized the idea that prompting models to write intermediate reasoning steps can unlock better performance on complex tasks.
guide_questions: Why does writing intermediate reasoning steps improve performance?
What kinds of tasks benefit most from CoT?
Why does CoT become more useful as models scale?
What is the difference between asking for an answer and asking for a reasoning path?
How would CoT change a Claude Code prompt for writing a PR article?
core_papers.business_translation: A good AI prompt is not just an instruction. It is a structure for how the model should think through the task.

core_papers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
core_papers.why_it_matters: It shows how base language models become instruction-following assistants through supervised fine-tuning and human preference optimization.
guide_questions: Why are bigger language models not automatically better at following user intent?
What are the three major steps in the InstructGPT training pipeline?
What does the reward model learn?
Why can a smaller InstructGPT be preferred over a much larger GPT-3?
What does this explain about Claude or ChatGPT tone and behavior?
core_papers.business_translation: Helpful AI behavior is trained, not accidental. Assistant models are base models shaped by human demonstrations, rankings, and safety preferences.

core_papers.title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
core_papers.why_it_matters: It studies how language models can learn when and how to call external tools such as calculators, search, and translation APIs.
guide_questions: What kinds of problems are better solved with tools than with model weights?
How does Toolformer generate training data for tool calls?
What does it mean for a model to decide whether a tool call is useful?
How is Toolformer related to Claude Code tool use?
What tools would a marketing agent need?
core_papers.business_translation: The future of AI work is not just smarter models. It is models that know when to call the right tool.

core_papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
core_papers.why_it_matters: It introduced the Transformer architecture, the foundation of modern LLMs.
guide_questions: What problem did attention solve compared with earlier sequence models?
Why does the Transformer process tokens differently from RNNs?
What is self-attention in plain language?
Why did this architecture scale so well?
Which parts are essential to understand, and which can be skipped for now?
core_papers.business_translation: Modern AI assistants are built on an architecture that lets models weigh relationships between words and concepts at scale.

core_papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
core_papers.why_it_matters: It showed that sufficiently large language models can perform many tasks from natural language instructions and a few examples without task-specific fine-tuning.
guide_questions: What is few-shot learning?
Why was GPT-3 a turning point for general-purpose language models?
What changed when tasks could be specified in prompts instead of training datasets?
What are the limits of few-shot prompting?
How does this connect to modern prompt workflows?
core_papers.business_translation: GPT-3 made it plausible that one general model could be steered into many business tasks through examples and instructions.

core_papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
core_papers.why_it_matters: It quantified how model performance improves with compute, dataset size, and parameter count.
guide_questions: What are scaling laws trying to predict?
Why did this paper encourage the industry to train larger models?
What does compute-optimal mean?
What does this paper not explain about model usefulness?
How should marketers read claims about larger models?
core_papers.business_translation: Scaling laws turned model building into an investment curve: more compute and data predictably improved loss, but not automatically product usefulness.

core_papers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
core_papers.why_it_matters: It argued many large models were undertrained and that compute-optimal training requires more data relative to parameters.
guide_questions: What did Chinchilla correct about the earlier scaling intuition?
Why can a smaller model outperform a larger undertrained model?
What is the relationship between parameters and training tokens?
How does this affect open model strategy?
What does this teach us about more vs better data?
core_papers.business_translation: Bigger is not enough. The model has to be trained with the right amount and quality of data for its size.

core_papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
core_papers.why_it_matters: It introduced a method for training AI assistants using explicit principles and AI-generated feedback, central to understanding Claude's design philosophy.
guide_questions: What is the constitution in Constitutional AI?
How is AI feedback different from human feedback?
Why does Anthropic care about harmlessness and helpfulness together?
How does this explain Claude's refusal behavior?
How would a brand or company define its own assistant constitution?
core_papers.business_translation: Assistant behavior can be shaped by explicit principles, not just examples. This is the bridge between model training and brand/policy behavior.

core_papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
core_papers.why_it_matters: It established the pattern of combining parametric model knowledge with retrieved external documents.
guide_questions: Why is retrieval needed if the model already has knowledge?
What is the difference between parametric and non-parametric memory?
What kinds of tasks benefit from RAG?
What can go wrong in retrieval?
How is RAG different from long context or personal memory?
core_papers.business_translation: RAG is how an AI system grounds its answer in external documents instead of relying only on what the model memorized during training.

core_papers.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
core_papers.why_it_matters: It frames LLM memory management like an operating system managing limited context and larger external storage.
guide_questions: Why is context window a memory bottleneck?
What does it mean for an LLM to manage memory explicitly?
How is MemGPT different from simple RAG?
Why is this relevant to Claude Memory or Obsidian workflows?
What should an AI assistant remember about Ann over months?
core_papers.business_translation: A useful AI employee needs memory management: what to keep in active context, what to store, and what to retrieve later.

core_papers.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
core_papers.why_it_matters: It shows how language agents can improve future attempts by converting failure feedback into verbal memory.
guide_questions: What kind of memory does Reflexion store?
How does verbal feedback help future attempts?
How is this different from model fine-tuning?
Where do we see this in Claude Code loops?
How could PaperGym use wrong answers to improve the next prescription?
core_papers.business_translation: Agents improve not only by retraining models, but by remembering what went wrong and changing the next attempt.

core_papers.title: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
core_papers.why_it_matters: It extends chain-of-thought from a single reasoning path to searching over multiple possible reasoning paths.
guide_questions: What is the difference between a chain and a tree of thoughts?
Why might multiple candidate reasoning paths help?
What does evaluation mean inside the search process?
When is ToT overkill?
How could proposal strategy work use a tree of options?
core_papers.business_translation: For complex decisions, AI should not just produce one answer. It should explore candidate paths, evaluate them, and choose.
